items.text: Next.js app with App Router – TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui.
Environment variables validation – Zod schema that validates all env vars at startup (src/lib/env.ts).
Database – Postgres, Drizzle ORM, connection pool, migration generation (drizzle-kit).
Redis – ioredis client, health check, used by rate limiter and queue.
Structured logging – pino logger with request ID middleware.
Global error handling – error.tsx, API error responses, Sentry (optional later).
Health check endpoints – /api/health (app), /api/health/db (pg), /api/health/redis.
Auth.js v5 – providers: GitHub, Google, Credentials (email/password).
Database adapter – Drizzle adapter for Auth.js (users, accounts, sessions).
Email verification – Resend for magic link / OTP (build abstraction, start with mock).
Role‑based access – role field (admin, author, user). Middleware/guard that checks role.
Protected routes – middleware.ts checks session, redirects to signin.
Sign in / Sign up pages – forms with client‑side validation (react-hook-form + zod).
User menu & session – avatar, name, dropdown (uses auth()).
Users – id, name, email, emailVerified, image, role, createdAt, stripeCustomerId, etc.
Accounts – for OAuth providers (Auth.js standard).
Sessions – database sessions.
VerificationTokens – for passwordless login.
Blog posts – id, title, slug, content (MDX string), excerpt, status (draft/published/scheduled), publishedAt, authorId, tags, etc.
Favorites – userId, itemType (blog/app), itemId, createdAt.
API keys – id, userId, name, key (hashed), scopes (array of appIds), usage limit, revoked, createdAt, lastUsedAt.
API usage logs – id, apiKeyId, appId, endpoint, timestamp, status, responseTime, ip.
Billing plans – id, name, price, features, stripePriceId (future).
Subscriptions – id, userId, planId, status, currentPeriodStart/End, stripeSubscriptionId.
Webhook endpoints – id, userId, url, events (array), secret, isActive.
Webhook delivery logs – id, webhookId, event, payload, status, createdAt.
GitHub projects (cached) – id, repoId, name, description, stars, language, url, updatedAt. (Could be fetched on‑the‑fly, but caching improves performance.)
Profile page – edit name, avatar, email preferences.
Favorites page – list favorite blog posts & apps, remove from list.
API keys management – create, revoke, copy to clipboard, see usage stats.
Billing page – show current plan (mock), upcoming invoice, usage (mock).
Quick access – a small floating panel or dropdown showing recent favorites for fast navigation.
User management – list all users, filter by role, change role, ban/delete.
Blog post management – list all posts, edit any, change status.
Analytics overview – integrate Google Analytics + Plausible stats (via their APIs), show page views, active users.
API metrics – total requests, per‑app usage, error rates (from usage logs).
Post creation – MDX editor (@uiw/react-md-editor), title, slug generation, tags, set status (draft/publish/schedule).
Post listing – public page with pagination, filtering by tag.
Post detail – render MDX server‑side via next-mdx-remote, code highlighting (rehype).
Scheduled publishing – BullMQ job that checks every minute for posts with status='scheduled' and publishedAt <= now, sets to published.
SEO for each post – dynamic metadata (title, description, OG image generated using sharp or @vercel/og).
Author role – only users with role author or admin can create/edit their posts.
Portfolio page – static content about you, skills, projects (managed via MDX files or admin panel).
GitHub projects page – server‑side fetch from GitHub API (using a personal access token), cache in Redis (TTL 1 hour), display grid of repos.
Markdown renderer for any project descriptions.
App pages – listing of all mini‑apps, individual app page with description and link to use it.
Image optimizer – your previous work, integrated as a client‑side tool or server action.
Sub‑navigation – each app section can have its own nav bar (contextual).
Internal API for apps – use Next.js API routes or Server Actions, protected by session (for your own use).
API key authentication – validate x-api-key header against database (hashed), attach user & scopes to request.
Rate limiting – token bucket per API key using Redis, configurable per plan.
API versioning – all public routes under /api/v1/.
Usage tracking – log every request (api_usage_logs), aggregate hourly/daily for dashboard.
API documentation – auto‑generate OpenAPI spec from route definitions (using a library or manual spec), render with Scalar at /docs.
Developer portal – page where external devs can view docs, get API key (if registered), see usage.
Webhook registration – UI in dashboard for users to add endpoint URLs, choose events (e.g., quota.exceeded, payment.success).
Webhook signature – generate a secret per endpoint, compute HMAC signature on delivery.
Delivery mechanism – when an event occurs, push job to BullMQ, worker sends HTTP POST to all matching webhooks, retry on failure.
Delivery logs – store status and response, expose in dashboard.
Add to favorites – button on blog posts and apps, calls internal API.
Quick access component – global dropdown/sidebar that shows last 5 favorites, using client component with useSession and fetch.
Favorites list – full page in dashboard.
Search index – create a materialized view or table search_index (id, type, title, body, tsvector).
Triggers – on blog insert/update, refresh the tsvector column.
Search endpoint – api/internal/search?q=... returns merged results (blog posts, maybe apps).
Search UI – input in global nav, results page.
Billing abstraction – BillingService interface: getPlans, getUserSubscription, createCheckoutSession, handleWebhook.
Mock implementation – returns static plans, always active free tier, no real charges.
Integration ready – environment variables for Stripe keys, webhook secret.
Future steps detailed – in the roadmap.
Environment config – NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID, NEXT_PUBLIC_PLAUSIBLE_DOMAIN.
Script injection – in root layout using next/script with strategies.
Custom events – track signups, blog views, API usage (optional).
Admin dashboard analytics – fetch GA4 data via Google Analytics Data API (server‑side) or embed reports; fetch Plausible stats via its API.
Tailwind CSS – base config, shadcn/ui components.
Dark mode – next-themes, toggle in global nav.
Responsive design – mobile‑first, nav collapse.
Metadata API – generateMetadata for all important pages.
Open Graph images – dynamic generation for blog posts using @vercel/og or sharp.
Sitemap – sitemap.ts at root that includes all public pages.
robots.txt – allow all, point to sitemap.
Local development with Docker Compose – docker-compose.yml (Postgres, Redis, Next.js dev server with hot reload).
Production Dockerfile – multi‑stage build, output standalone Next.js.
Coolify setup – point to GitHub repo, set environment variables, auto‑deploy on push.
GitHub Actions – optional, lint & test before deploy (can be integrated with Coolify).
Database migrations in CI – run drizzle-kit migrate on deploy.
Vitest configuration – unit tests for lib functions.
React Testing Library – smoke tests for critical pages (signin, blog).
API route tests – using testcontainers or in‑memory fetch.
E2E (optional) – Playwright for signup + blog creation flow.
BullMQ – job queue backed by Redis.
Worker process – separate container/process that handles email sending, webhook deliveries, scheduled publishing.
Cron – node-cron in worker to enqueue scheduled publishing checks, backup, etc.
Database backup script – dump to file, upload to R2 using @aws-sdk/client-s3.
Cron schedule – daily at 3 AM via worker process or Coolify cron.
